title: Classic British Peach Pie
description: A traditional British dessert featuring peaches in a sweet and flaky pie crust. Perfect for warm summer days or as a comforting treat any time of the year.
instructions: Roll out the puff pastry dough to a thickness of about 1/8 inch.
Place the sliced peaches in the center of the dough, leaving a 1-inch border around the edges.
Dust the peaches with a little mash and sprinkle with brown sugar.
Brush the edges of the dough with the beaten egg for a golden glaze.
Fold the dough over the peaches to form a pie crust, pressing the edges to seal.
Cut a few slits in the top crust to allow steam to escape.
Brush the top crust with the remaining egg wash.
Place the pie on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and bake for 40-45 minutes, or until the crust is golden brown.
Let the pie cool for 10-15 minutes before serving.
